meta.note: Egypt (deepen) + Kenya (beyond Ngũgĩ) sweep, Tier 1 high-confidence + Tier 2 medium. Each candidate deduped against the DB and re-sourced. Tier 3 thin AFTE list-mentions and Kinyatti author-suppression held; 'So As Not To Be Hit...' (no author) and 'Complete Works of Gibran' (not a discrete title) excluded.

entries.title: That Smell
entries.description_ban: All copies were seized and the book banned by the government immediately on publication in 1966; the chief censor objected to an explicit scene with a prostitute. Now a recognised landmark of modern Arabic literature.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Landmark Egyptian censorship case (Tilka al-Ra'iha); documented in literary press.

entries.title: Where Is God?
entries.description_ban: Security forces confiscated all copies of this short-story collection about poor farmers (Ayna Allah?); Saber was sentenced in absentia to five years for 'contempt of religion' (Beba court, May 2013).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Contempt-of-religion conviction and confiscation; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: Responsibility for the Failure of the Islamic State
entries.description_ban: Al-Azhar's Islamic Research Council recommended banning and confiscation of this reformist work for 'differing from the consensus of religious scholars.'
entries.inclusion_rationale: Al-Azhar-driven religious ban; reported by the Christian Science Monitor.

entries.title: Studies in Muslim Apocalyptic
entries.description_ban: Al-Azhar's Islamic Research Academy ordered the banning and confiscation of this scholarly work, deeming it damaging to Islam's image.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Al-Azhar ban of a Western academic work; reported via Morningstar/Dow Jones.

entries.title: The Scientific Report
entries.description_ban: Al-Azhar's Islamic Research Academy stopped distribution of its own commissioned book after Copts condemned it for defaming Christianity; it had been released with the Academy's magazine.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Al-Azhar withdrawal over inter-religious offence; reported by the Los Angeles Times.

entries.title: The Angel: The Egyptian Spy Who Saved Israel
entries.description_ban: Publisher Khaled Lotfy was jailed five years by a military court for printing the Arabic edition ('divulging military secrets'); his Tanmiya publishing house was barred from the Cairo Book Fair.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Military-court suppression and publisher imprisonment; documented by AFTE.
entries.source_name: AFTE (Association for Freedom of Thought and Expression)

entries.title: Homethology: Controversy of Identity and Myth
entries.description_ban: All copies were confiscated from the 54th Cairo International Book Fair (2023), reportedly by intelligence services, over claims it attributed Egyptian civilization to Sudan.
entries.source_name: AFTE (Association for Freedom of Thought and Expression)

entries.title: The Taming of Tyranny
entries.description_ban: Pulled from shelves into closed cartons at the 54th Cairo Book Fair (2023) on an unofficial order, despite having passed review and obtained registration numbers.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Book-fair restriction; documented by AFTE (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: AFTE (Association for Freedom of Thought and Expression)

entries.title: The New Dictatorship
entries.description_ban: Pulled from shelves into closed cartons at the 54th Cairo Book Fair (2023) on an unofficial order, despite having passed review and obtained registration numbers.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Book-fair restriction; documented by AFTE (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: AFTE (Association for Freedom of Thought and Expression)

entries.title: Horgard
entries.description_ban: The Publications Censorship Agency banned this novel in 2011; the move drew an internal protest from a Supreme Constitutional Court official demanding the Agency's dissolution.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Censorship-agency ban; documented by AFTE (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: AFTE (Association for Freedom of Thought and Expression)

entries.title: The History of the Zionist Movement and Its Organizations
entries.description_ban: Removed from a pavilion at the 54th Cairo Book Fair (2023) on a security demand, then quietly returned after a social-media solidarity campaign.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Book-fair restriction (later reversed); documented by AFTE (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: AFTE (Association for Freedom of Thought and Expression)

entries.title: Kenya: Return to Reason
entries.description_ban: Memoir/political tract by FORD-Asili opposition leader Kenneth Matiba, critical of Moi's rule. Banned by a special issue of the official gazette in January 1994 after security personnel raided the printer and seized thousands of copies.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Gazette banning order of an opposition book; reported by UPI and The Independent.

entries.title: After 4:30
entries.description_ban: Bestselling pulp narrative poem of Nairobi nightlife; among Maillu's Comb Books titles banned by the Kenyatta government in 1976 for sexually explicit content.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Government obscenity ban of popular pulp fiction; reported by the Daily Nation (medium confidence).

entries.title: My Dear Bottle
entries.description_ban: Maillu's confessional verse novel; part of the popular Comb Books output banned in the 1976 government crackdown on his sexually explicit pocket novels.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Government obscenity ban of popular pulp fiction; reported by the Daily Nation (medium confidence).

entries.title: Independent Kenya
entries.description_ban: Anonymous Marxist critique of post-independence Kenya, issued underground as 'Cheche Kenya' (1981) then published by Zed Press, London (1982). Proscribed in Kenya; possession was treated as sedition under Moi.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Proscribed dissident book of the Moi era; documented in academic literature on Kenyan underground publishing (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: Emerald — The other Kenya: underground and alternative literature
